seoTitle: New Park vs New Valley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between New Park and New Valley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: New Park vs New Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in New Park and New Valley.
heading: New Park vs New Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between New Park, New South Wales and New Valley, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: New Park has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to New Valley (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is New Park or New Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: New Park has a flood risk score of 24/100, while New Valley scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: New Park scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. New Valley scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in New Park or New Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. New Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
